entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Dino C D and I'm a Cybersecurity Graduate with a Passion for IoT, AI
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Dino C D and I'm a postgraduate student pursuing my MSc in Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University, BYC, Bengaluru. I completed my Bachelor of Computer Applications at St Joseph's University, Bengaluru, where I built a strong foundation in programming, cloud deployment, full-stack development, and IoT engineering.

 

Beyond academics, I'm a multi-award-winning innovator (3× 1st Prize for my IoT IV Blood Detector Device), a patent applicant, an active cybersecurity community contributor through S.H.I.E.L.D, and a researcher working on audio deepfake detection. I love building things that solve real problems — whether that's a smart medical device, an interactive history website, or a security-aware web application.

Pitching my patent-pending IoT IV Blood Detector to a C-DAC judge

 

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a leading cybersecurity engineer who doesn't just defend systems — but actively shapes the way security is built into technology from the ground up. I want to work at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, developing intelligent threat detection systems that can anticipate attacks before they happen.

On a bigger scale, I want to contribute to making healthcare technology safer. My IoT IV Blood Detector project opened my eyes to how vulnerable critical medical infrastructure can be. My dream is to see a world where hospital systems, patient data, and medical IoT devices are protected by design — and I want to be part of building that future. A patent in progress feels like the first small step toward that dream.

 

 

 

 

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself working as a SOC Analyst or Cybersecurity Researcher at a technology or cybersecurity firm — ideally contributing to threat intelligence, incident response, or AI-powered security tooling. I hope to have completed my MSc, earned certifications like CompTIA Security+ and CEH, and published at least one peer-reviewed research paper (starting with my current work on audio deepfake detection).

I also see myself mentoring the next wave of cybersecurity students — much like the mentors and communities that shaped me. If the journey goes the way I hope, perhaps I'll be sharing a very different blog post on the SAP Community in 2031, looking back at how it all started. 😊

 

Why did you choose MSc Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University?

Honestly, it felt like the perfect convergence of everything I was already passionate about. After my BCA, I knew I wanted to go deeper — not just into cybersecurity, but into how AI is reshaping the entire threat landscape. Christ University's programme offered exactly that combination.

What truly won me over was the learning environment. The workshops have been eye-opening — hands-on, practical, and constantly pushing you to think beyond textbooks. The classes don't just teach concepts, they challenge you to question, build, and apply. Every session feels like it's preparing you for a problem that actually exists in the real world.

I'm genuinely grateful for the exposure this course has given me — from AI-driven security techniques to research opportunities. It's not just a degree; it's the foundation I needed to become the cybersecurity professional I want to be. 🎓


What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

Without a doubt: Temporal Explorer — Past to Future.

The idea was simple but ambitious — build an interactive website that lets users travel through time, from ancient civilisations all the way to speculative futures. We used HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create animated timelines, artefact showcases, and immersive storytelling experiences.

What made it so fun wasn't just the tech — it was the creativity involved. We debated what the future might look like, researched historical artefacts, and designed transitions that felt like you were genuinely moving through eras. Completing it in time for the Web Weavers intercollegiate competition and walking away with 2nd place made it even more rewarding. It reminded me that the best projects aren't just technically sound — they tell a story.


What is your hobby?

My biggest hobby is community work and volunteering. Over the past few years, I've coordinated 50+ outreach events with AICUF, taught constitutional literacy in government schools through KJES, and helped build the cybersecurity awareness culture at Christ University through S.H.I.E.L.D.

It might sound unusual as a "hobby," but there's something deeply energising about connecting with people, solving real-world problems together, and seeing the direct impact of your effort — whether that's a student who finally understands their civic rights, or a community that feels a little more supported. It keeps me grounded in why I'm pursuing technology in the first place: to serve people.

A volunteering Social work which was held in Biccodu, Karnataka by KJES

 


What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic — AI & Cybersecurity?

The future is fascinating — and a little terrifying, honestly. Here's my prediction:

AI will become the most powerful weapon in both attack and defence simultaneously. We're already seeing AI-generated phishing emails that are indistinguishable from real ones, deepfake audio used in fraud (which is exactly why I'm researching it), and autonomous malware that adapts in real time. The adversarial AI race is already here.

On the defence side, I believe AI-powered SOC platforms will eventually handle 90% of threat triage autonomously — flagging only the most complex incidents for human analysts. Security will shift from "detect and respond" to "predict and prevent."

The wildcard? IoT security. Billions of connected devices — many with zero security baked in — will be the biggest attack surface of the next decade. My IV detector project taught me first-hand how exposed medical IoT can be. I believe the next generation of cybersecurity professionals will spend as much time securing physical devices as they do securing data.

The future belongs to those who understand both the intelligence of AI and the vulnerabilities it creates.


Share a fun fact about yourself that people don't know!

I once helped coordinate a community event managing ₹2.5 lakh in organisational expenditure — and I was a second-year undergrad student at the time. 

As the core committee member for CHRONICLES (AICUF's major fest at St Joseph's University), I was suddenly responsible for budgeting, resource allocation, vendor coordination, and keeping everything on track. No one tells you that community leadership is basically a crash course in project management, crisis handling, and people skills — all at once.

Oh, and I did all of that while simultaneously building AWS deployments, learning Kali Linux, and developing IoT devices in my spare time. Sleep was optional, apparently.

With Justice Kurian Joseph, Retired Judge of the Supreme Court of India — Chief Guest at CHRONICLES, St Joseph's University, Bengaluru. ⚖️

 

And now let's hear from you!

What do you imagine or predict is the future of AI and Cybersecurity — and how are you preparing for it? Drop your thoughts in the comments below — I'd love to hear where you think the field is heading! 🚀

Dino C D | MSc AI & Cybersecurity Student, Christ University, Bengaluru
🔗linkedin.com/in/dinocd | 💻 github.com/Dino20004

entry.title: Re: it is possible to create a purchase order using a div...
entry.content:
Good day @arnaudf 

Thank you for your questions.

The key reason why the plant is blocked is, plant and division are treated differently in the organisational model. Sales structures are linked to Finance through company code and to procurement through plant, while division belongs to the Sales and Distribution organisational setup.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/88d0329957b5473e95880e1c09880e3c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c473e4fb3bca4420badc0de89c520328/ef0a6270dae145d68340cadf9e31f031-26.html?locale=en-US

Because of this, when you change the company code in your purchase order, the system performs a consistency validation for procurement relevant data such as plant, which is why the plant mismatch is blocked. I am not aware of any hard validation for division against company code when you save PO, which explains why you can still save the document in your test.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/2187ec203ad74f578f2aa2e6cf3accad.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/38cbf557c328be12e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

So, your result is system driven rather than an application error. If your business requires a stricter check for division after company code change, that would be an additional requirement rather than something covered by the standard PO validation logic.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content:
I think, it cannot be a problem on SAP side, because if I understand the scenario correctly, then SAP is the sender, and BizTalk the receiver, right? ARFCRSTATE is used on receiver side ("R"), while if it were a problem on sender side, the table would be ARFCSSTATE ("S" for sender).

Also SM58 usually displays the error message coming back from the receiver side (BizTalk/NCo), so it looks like the error is happening there. As I said: the RFC trace on BizTalk/NCo side should show more details.

Thinking a bit more about it, something like this must be happening: SAP sends a tRFC (which includes the State table, which in turn contains the transaction ID (TID)). But when the call arrives on NCo side, NCo notices that that table is empty. So either it is indeed a problem on SAP side (SAP accidentally sending empty/incorrect transaction data), or another idea that just comes to my mind: is the SM59 destination used for sending these IDocs set to "Non-Unicode"? This could cause all kinds of data corruption problems. (And this could also be seen in an RFC trace...)

If this is the case, set it to "Unicode", delete all old IDocs in SM58 (because they have been recorded in Non-Unicode format) and then try a fresh IDoc.

entry.title: Re: 1010 Balance Carryforward - Divestured Consolidation...
entry.content:
Good day @FranConde 

Thank you for your questions.

Based on SAP’s standard design, the system does not provide separate automatic task that will carry forward reserves of a divested consolidation unit as opening balances of that same divested unit in the following year.

The following are the reasons that happens:

  • First, balance Carryforward pull balances of relevant FS items from the previous fiscal year into the current fiscal year. Which also confirms LEQ and AST FS items being always carried forward as a business requirement.
  • Second, total Divestiture is used to invert all data previously posted in the consolidation of investments for the divested investee. So, only balance sheet or statistical items are reversed and protected items such as retained earnings are treated via the consolidation of investments clearing logic.
  • Third, for loss of control/total divestiture, the system does not undo consolidation group-dependent entries.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/86b7e4024e4b4264891ce4d377ca075c.html?locale=en-US

In summary, this means the system does not automatically interpret “reserves that should remain in group equity after divestiture” as “opening balances that should stay on the divested consolidation unit next year”. So, in standard design, once the unit is divested, the expectation should usually be that those amounts are either:

  1. Reversed as part of divestiture logic or
  2. Explicitly retained at group level by reclassification, not left on the divested unit as its new-year opening balance.

 

Question: Are you missing any any automatic task or setup that manages this point?

Answer: No additional task is required for this exact point. The standard relevant tasks are:

  • For you to maintain the divestiture in the consolidation hierarchy
  • Then run the appropriate Total Divestiture/consolidation group change processing in the divestiture year
  • And finally run Balance Carryforward for the year change.

 

Considering there is none, the best practice for this point is:

  • For you not to expect the divested consolidation unit itself to carry those reserves forward into the next year once it has left the group. That is not the standard conceptual target of BCF after divestiture.
  • If your reserves from realised profits must remain in group equity, then the better practice is for you to reclassify them into a surviving group equity position rather than leave them on the divested unit.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/86b7e4024e4b4264891ce4d377ca075c.html?locale=en-US

  • So, in practice, this would usually mean the following:
    • Complete the divestiture processing in the year of divestment
    • Analyse which balance must remain in consolidated equity
    • Post a manual Group Reporting journal entry (using Import Group Journal Entries) to move the relevant reserve balance to the appropriate parent or group-level FS item before or after year-end, depending on your accounting design and reporting timeline.

So, the likely best practice is for you to manually reclassify your reserve to the continuing group structure, not expecting BCF to keep it on the divested unit.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/a2d5433ad6b045c99b2ec8142b09f097.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (Poznań, Poland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need any background in the topic, but you should be highly curious and have some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event planned for Wednesday, April 22, in Poznań, Poland 🇵🇱 from 10:00 to 16:00 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is hosted by Capgemini at
Business Garden Poznań
ul. Kolorowa 6
60-198 Poznań
1st floor, room Borneo

Please note that space is limited; we can accommodate only 25 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for business information embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python , in this case

entry.title: Re: HTTP failed: INTERNAL_ERROR in SAP Gateway Client for...
entry.content: Hello, The issue was not related to the CDS view or OData configuration. In my case the problem was caused by the host name used by the Gateway Client. The system tried to call http://sap:8000/..., but the host sap was not resolvable (no DNS/hosts entry). Because of this, the request failed before reaching the Gateway service. Calling the service with localhost solved the issue. Example: Incorrect: http://sap:8000/sap/opu/odata/sap/<service_name>/$metadata Working: http://localhost:8000/sap/opu/odata/sap/<service_name>/$metadata I was testing a CDS OData service created in the $TMP package.

entry.title: Re: Enable Offline Store for Online OData Service in SAP MDK
entry.content:
To add to Bills comment, You should be careful with trying to store or combine data across services. Data from the online service does not automatically get saved into the offline DB, and records cannot be "linked" across destinations/services. Ideally if you want something offline, you can use the Online search to find the object and then check the object key into the dependancy queue (for SSAM) for the offline service to then download this object. then the user can interact with the object in offline mode. The offline and online structure & data should be mirrored so that inconsistencies do not arise.

Most of the Data in SSAM online service is also available in the offline Service. SSAM is offline first by design. So you should have access to the Data offline then options to use the online search for additional data, which can then be downloaded. (Equip, Floc, unplanned parts, Goods movemnts etc.. )

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRVHello, I am integrating Supplier Invoice da
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am integrating Supplier Invoice data using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using API odatav2 API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV.

 

While trying to post the supplier invoice, in some cases, i am facing problemas related to unit measure when using the navigation to_SuplrInvcItemPurOrdRef.

 

Here a payload example on this navigation:

 

<d:SupplierInvoiceItem>1</d:SupplierInvoiceItem>

<d:PurchaseOrder>100002268</d:PurchaseOrder>

entry.title: Issue with Delivery Creation Date Calculation in PO
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am currently facing an issue while creating a Purchase Order (PO). Even though the delivery date is properly maintained in the PO, the system is calculating the delivery creation date as a past date based on the planned delivery time.

Normally, the delivery creation date should be calculated as PO creation date + planned delivery time, resulting in a future date. However, in this case, it appears that the system is calculating it as PO creation date – planned delivery time.

Due to this behavior, users are encountering difficulties while creating outbound deliveries through VL10D, as the PO is not appearing in the list. This seems to be because the delivery date in the system is being updated as a past date.

Could anyone please help identify the reason for this behavior and suggest how this issue can be resolved?

Your support would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to use BTP destination with OAuth2ClientCredentia...
entry.content:
Hi Rayman,

You don’t necessarily need a manual CAP approach for this. The BTP Destination service can handle the token exchange automatically if configured correctly. Based on your description, here are a few things you should check:

Authentication Type: In your BTP Destination configuration, ensure the Authentication is set to OAuth2ClientCredentials. This tells the service to fetch the token using the client ID and secret before forwarding the request.

App Router Config: In your xs-app.json, make sure the route to your destination has authenticationMethod: "route".

Service Binding: The "Invalid access token" error often happens if the App Router and the Destination service are not bound to the same XSUAA instance. Check your mta.yaml to ensure both are using the same security provider.

Local vs Cloud: If you are testing via BAS preview, it sometimes struggles with token propagation. Try deploying the app to Cloud Foundry; the Managed App Router usually resolves these token issues in the live environment.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Not able to change the flexible Workflow once it is a...

For me the accepted answer didn't work. What worked for me was: 

  1. Find your workflow template (WS...) if you don’t know it.
  2. Run SWETYPV, filter:
    • Object Type: YOUR_ABAP_CLASS
    • Event: START_WF
  3. Note the receiver workflow template (WS...).
  4. Run PFTC_CHG.
  5. Choose Workflow Template and open that WS... template.

Enter it without WS.

entry.title: Re: I am facing errors in opening the Build Apps Editor.
entry.content:
Hi Ananthu,

It seems like you've already tried the standard troubleshooting steps (cache, incognito, and boosters). Since you mentioned a "Backend Database" error earlier, it’s possible there is a sync issue between your Subaccount and the Build Apps tenant.

Before concluding it's a platform-wide outage, could you please check these two specific things:

Role Collection Assignment: Sometimes just assigning the role isn't enough. Ensure you have the BuildAppsAdmin (for development) and RegistryAdmin (for backend) role collections. Try removing and re-assigning them to your user, then log out and log back in to refresh the session.

Custom Identity Provider (IAS): Are you using a custom IAS or the default SAP ID Service? If it's a custom IdP, ensure that the "Attributes" (like email and groups) are being passed correctly in the SAML assertion, as Build Apps is very sensitive to user attributes.

Check Service Status: You can check the SAP Trust Center to see if there is an active incident for SAP Build Apps in your specific region.

If the issue persists despite these checks, it might indeed be a backend synchronization error on the SAP side, and raising a support ticket under the component CA-LCA would be the best next step.

Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Test Data Refresh service request on SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hello Tobias Koebler / TDR team,

SAP recommends to plan the TDR 'outside business hours' (to avoid data inconsistencies, which we had after our previous TDR). But the TDR starts on Monday / Tuesday and SAP takes 24 hours to refresh the data. So how can this be 'outside business hours'?

Our question: Is the selected start time exactly the time that PRD activity should be minimized to avoid data corruption? And how long will it take?  

SAP reply (S/4HANA Cloud Operations): Yes. As per best practices, we need 24 hours window to complete the data transfer. But if the data transfer completed earlier, we will update the same to you.

As companies usually do not close for 24 hours during week days the advice of planning 'outside business hours' does not make sense.  Please advise!

Thank you, Boris

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements List Report: A Guide to Displaying and Filtering Business Data
entry.content:
What is a List Report in SAP Fiori Elements?

List Report is a Fiori Elements template that displays business data in a table with filtering, searching, sorting, and navigation capabilities.

It is usually the main entry page of a Fiori application, where users can see multiple records and perform actions like filtering, sorting, or navigating to detailed information.

Key Components of a List Report:
          1.Smart Filter Bar---> Used to search and filter data.

          2. Table (List of Records)---> Displays the business data in rows and columns.

Flow Of List Report:

1. CDS View
               ↓
2. UI Annotations
                ↓
3. OData Service (V2 / V4)
                 ↓
4. SAP Fiori Elements List Report Template
                 ↓
5. SAP Fiori Application UI


1. How to Create CDS View:

Open your ABAP Project in Eclipse (ADT), then right-click on the project or package, choose New, and select ABAP Repository Object.

Click on ABAP Repository Object. Under Core Data Services, select Data Definition. Then provide the required details such as Package Name, CDS View Name, and Description, and proceed to create the CDS view.

Present I  was saving my cds view in local package for that reason  TR list are not visuable , othe wise all  TR 's will showing  under the  Transport Request  .

CAfter creating the CDS View, check the syntax, activate the object, and execute the CDS View to verify the data.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZLIST_R'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'List Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ZCDS_List_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as CreationDate,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}

2. UI Annotations : 

You can add annotations directly in the same CDS View, or you can create them separately using a Metadata Extension.

Explanation

In SAP CDS development, annotations can be maintained in two ways:

  1. Inside the Same CDS View
    You define the annotations directly within the CDS view definition.

       2.Using a Metadata Extension
         You create a separate Metadata Extension object to maintain UI annotations.
         This keeps the data model and UI annotations separated.

       Metadata Extension Creation:

Important Note:  Whenever you create a Metadata Extension, you must add the following annotation in the main CDS View to allow the extension.

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

Without adding this annotation in the main CDS View, the Metadata Extension will not work for that CDS object.

  Main CDS View  & Metadata Extension View Code:

1. Main CDS View
____________________

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZLIST_R'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'List Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view ZCDS_List_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
      
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
   
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as CreationDate, 
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
__________________________________
2.Meta Data Extension View:
_________________________________

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@Search.searchable: true
annotate entity ZCDS_List_Report with
{
 @search.defaultSearchElement: true
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 10}]
  @ui.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
  SalesDocument;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 20}]
  @ui.selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 30}]
  @ui.selectionField: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 40}]
  DistrChannel;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 50}]
  Amount;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 60}]
  Currency;
  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 70}]
  CreationDate;

  @ui.lineItem: [{position: 80,label: 'Month Year'}]
  @ui.selectionField: [{ position: 40  }]
  Month_Year;

}

You can add annotations directly in the same CDS View, or you can create them separately using a Metadata Extension. It is your choice based on the business requirement and design approach.

3. OData Service (V2 / V4) :

 Consume a CDS View in OData Services (ABAP):

1.  Using  @odata.publish: true 

2.   Using SEGW Tcode 

In this section, I will demonstrate how to consume a CDS View using the SEGW transaction code (SAP Gateway Service Builder).

In this document, I am not covering the complete OData service creation process step by step. I will only demonstrate how to consume a CDS View in an OData service.

 

4. SAP Fiori Elements List Report Template

Go to the SAP BTP Business Application Studio (BAS) and follow the steps shown in the screenshot below.

The above screenshot is for your reference. In the OData service, the Service Name and Entity will appear in the SAP BTP Business Application Studio (BAS) tool as shown in the screenshot below.

 

 

5. SAP Fiori Application UI

The Fiori Elements List Report appears as shown in the screenshot below. It provides a filter bar for searching and filtering records, and the business data is displayed in a tabular format.

     

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: How SAP Joule Will Transform User Support in the Fieldglass Landscape
entry.content:
Anyone who has spent years working with SAP Fieldglass—whether on the customer side running a large MSP program or, like me today, as a Principal Functional Delivery Consultant at SAP—knows one recurring challenge all too well: users who only interact with the system occasionally.

Hiring managers, project leads, timesheet approvers, budget owners—roles like these often touch Fieldglass only sporadically. Yet this exact user group generates a disproportionately high volume of support needs. And the reasons are easy to understand:

 

  1. Low familiarity with the system,
  2. Changing requirements,
  3. Process complexity,
  4. and despite well‑written user guides, a consistently high demand for hands‑on help.

 

Why these users—and the PMOs supporting them—will soon see massive relief

With SAP Joule, the generative AI assistant embedded deeply within the SAP ecosystem, the Fieldglass domain is about to experience a significant shift. Many of the inquiries that today require manual intervention by PMOs, administrators, or program support teams will soon be handled directly and intelligently by Joule.

And this, in my view, is the real breakthrough:

Anything that is done rarely benefits tremendously from conversational, guided assistance.

As AI adoption accelerates across enterprises, the willingness of users to rely on an AI assistant is growing rapidly—making Joule’s impact in Fieldglass almost inevitable.

 

Everyday Use Cases Joule Will Effortlessly Take Over

The list of tasks that Joule can simplify or automate in Fieldglass is extensive. Here are some of the most common scenarios from real project experience:

 

  1. “Where can I find my timesheet?”

A timeless classic.

Despite simple navigation, many users inevitably get lost—or look in the wrong place.

Joule will:

 

  1. provide the exact link to the timesheet,
  2. explain its status (“submitted”, “rejected”, “awaiting approval”),
  3. and suggest the next action when necessary.

 

  1. Template questions (Job Postings, SOWs, Work Orders)

Tasks done only a few times a year always produce questions.

Joule will:

 

  1. propose the right template based on role and context,
  2. explain its use case,
  3. and clarify differences between template variations.

 

  1. Process guidance: “How do I adjust a worker’s rate?”, “How do I extend a Work Order?”

These are frequent questions, but often detailed and nuanced.

Joule will:

 

  1. walk users through every step,
  2. highlight relevant configurations,
  3. and prevent typical mistakes.

 

  1. Interpreting and resolving error messages

Many support tickets stem from misunderstanding simple system warnings or validations.

Joule will:

 

  1. analyze the error in real time,
  2. explain the cause in plain language,
  3. and offer immediate solutions.

 

  1. Navigation support

Especially in global organizations with complex roles and views, users can quickly feel overwhelmed.

Joule will:

 

  1. provide contextual guidance,
  2. explain menus and actions,
  3. and reduce the learning curve dramatically.

 

 

Why Joule Is More Than a Convenience Feature

From years of Fieldglass implementations and customer advisory roles, I see three major benefits:

  1. Significant workload reduction for PMO and admin teams

A large portion of repetitive, manual support work will diminish—allowing teams to focus on strategic improvements rather than daily troubleshooting.

  1. Substantial improvements in user experience

Infrequent users can interact confidently, complete tasks faster, and avoid frustration.

  1. Fewer mistakes, fewer tickets, smoother operations

Improved guidance means fewer process errors, fewer escalations, and overall higher data quality.

 

Looking Ahead: Why Joule Will Be a Natural Success in Fieldglass

I am convinced that the combination of real‑world usefulness, intuitive interaction, and rising enterprise trust in AI will make Joule’s adoption in Fieldglass almost automatic.

Organizations that embrace it early will:

 

  1. reduce internal support load,
  2. accelerate task completion,
  3. and empower their users with on‑demand, context‑aware guidance.

 

And with time, as Joule becomes increasingly knowledgeable about Fieldglass configurations, processes, and customer-specific setups, the assistant won’t just answer questions—it will support true automation.

 

Conclusion

Joule is poised to fundamentally change how users engage with SAP Fieldglass—especially those who rarely touch the system.

For PMO teams, administrators, and support organizations, this means less manual work, fewer repetitive questions, and fewer tickets.

For end users, it means greater clarity, faster navigation, and more successful outcomes.

The potential is enormous—and we are only at the beginning.

entry.title: Re: Best way to learn Capm in real time
entry.content:
Hello Divya,

Learning CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) with Node.js is best approached by combining official theory with hands-on practice. Since you are looking for real-time learning, I recommend this structured path:

SAP Learning Journey: Start with the official Building Applications with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model journey. It’s free and covers the fundamentals of CDS (Core Data Services) and Node.js integration.

The "Bookshop" Sample: As mentioned by others, the Capire (official documentation) "Bookshop" application is the gold standard. Instead of just following it, try adding custom logic using Node.js handlers (srv.before, srv.on, srv.after) to understand how real-time data processing works.

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS): Don’t just code locally. Use SAP BAS to get familiar with the cloud-native dev environment, including HDI container deployment and SAP HANA Cloud integration.

Hands-on Tutorials: Follow the SAP Developers Center tutorials. Specifically, look for "Create a Full-Stack Application with CAP" which connects Fiori elements with the CAP backend.

Community Blogs: Follow the #CAP tag on SAP Community. Real-world troubleshooting and advanced patterns (like JWT authentication or External Service integration) are best learned through peer blogs.

Pro-tip: Focus heavily on CDS modeling first, as it is the backbone of CAP. Once your data model is solid, writing the Node.js logic becomes much more intuitive.

Happy learning! 

Regards,

Janvi kapoor

entry.title: Re: Superpackage ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid package
entry.content:
Good day @Karthik_Gali 

Thank you for your questions.

That issue you described is caused by a mismatch between the package hierarchy you originally used for development and the hierarchy now expected for transport. SAP’s Public Cloud ABAP lifecycle distinguishes ZPARTNER and ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT. Which means objects cannot be moved between those two package trees. This explains both the transport inconsistency error and the message that ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid superpackage for the existing package.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/49b78cc3ac4240048cacb179b2a49403.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/abap-packages?locale=en-US

The practical option Public Cloud supports is for you to create a new package under the correct transportable structure package, which is typically ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT if that is the software component you used for QA import and then manually copy or recreated the custom objects there. After that, you can transport the new package content through the normal Public Cloud extensibility process.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/ec943b4ee4ac4dfdac47081d1d3e4019.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/483fff16519b48c5a37a65d845a50624.html?locale=en-US

Here is a summary:

  1. Ensure that your development objects are created in packages that are sub-packages of ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT to maintain the correct software component and transport layer settings.
  2. If you have objects in the ZLOCAL package, you need to reassign them to a package under ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT.  You can do this by creating a new transport request and assigning it to the new package.
  3. If the transport request is released, you can reassign the objects from the ZLOCAL package to the new package under ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT.
  4. Note that it is not possible to move objects between packages that reside in different transportable software component or between ZLOCAL and ZCUSOM_DEVELOPMENT.

Always ensure that you uniquely name your objects to avoid naming conflicts. If you encounter an object collision, then delete the conflicting object and create it anew in the correct package.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL Schema Evolution in SAP CAP - How to Handl...
entry.content:
Hello Medelkouch,

This is a fantastic contribution to the CAP ecosystem! Dealing with Schema Evolution in PostgreSQL while using @cap-js/postgres has always been a bit of a "handle with care" situation, especially because the default cds deploy focuses on safety over flexibility.

The gap you've identified is very real. In production environments, manual SQL migrations or handling "Schema Drift" manually not only increases the risk of human error but also breaks the CI/CD automation flow.

Why this solution is impressive:

Zero-Downtime: This is a game-changer. Most developers struggle with the "stop-app-migrate-start-app" cycle.

Delta Detection: Automating the comparison between CSN (Cloud Service Network) files and the deployed schema is exactly what’s missing in the standard toolkit for destructive operations.

Safety via pgroll: Borrowing the "expand and contract" pattern (inspired by Shopify/pgroll) is the industry standard for safe migrations, and bringing that to CAP is brilliant.

A quick question for the implementation: How does @CDS-pgroll/postgres handle the MTA (Multi-Target Application) deployment flow? Is it integrated as a custom hook in the mta.yaml deployer task, or do we need to run it as a separate sidecar?

I'll definitely be checking out the GitHub repo. This looks like a must-have utility for anyone running enterprise-scale CAP apps on PostgreSQL.

Keep up the great work!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Delivery Creation Date Calculation in PO
entry.content:
Hello Hassan,

The behavior you are describing usually occurs because of Backward Scheduling. In SAP, the system starts from the "Delivery Date" you entered and subtracts the "Planned Delivery Time" (PDT) and "GR Processing Time" to find the "Purchase Order Date."

If the result is a past date, the system flags it, which is why your PO might not be showing up correctly in VL10D.

Here are a few things you should check to resolve this:

Check Scheduling Direction: In the customizing for Purchasing, check if the system is set to Backward or Forward scheduling. If the delivery date is fixed and PDT is long, the system will always calculate a past date for order placement.

Planned Delivery Time (PDT): Check the PDT in the Material Master (MRP 2 view) or the Purchasing Info Record. If the PDT is set to a very high number of days, the system will subtract those days from your Delivery Date, pushing the "Creation Date" into the past.

VL10D Selection Criteria: In VL10D, check the "Delivery Creation Date" range in the selection screen. If the system has calculated a past date, you need to expand your date range to include that past date to see the PO in the list.

Rescheduling Check: Try changing the Delivery Date in the PO to a much further future date. If the "Creation Date" then becomes a current or future date, it confirms that the system is using Backward Scheduling based on your PDT.

Recommendation: Check your Info Record first, as often a wrong PDT value there overrides the Material Master and causes this calculation error.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Service instances stuck in "Deletion in Progress" on...
entry.content:
Hello Arul,

This is a classic "Orphaned Service" issue on BTP Trial. Since the main HANA Cloud instance is already gone, the Service Broker is unable to reach the database to finish the deletion, leaving your instances stuck.

Since you've already tried standard cf delete, here is the most effective way to fix this:

Check for Service Bindings: Most instances get stuck because of active bindings. Make sure to delete all Service Bindings or Service Keys first via the BTP Cockpit UI. If they are also stuck, try deleting the Application they are bound to.

Force Delete via Terminal: If cf delete-service fails, try the -f (force) flag specifically:
cf delete-service -f

The Last Resort (Space Deletion): Since this is a Trial account, the quickest way to clear "Deletion in Progress" instances is to Delete the entire Space ('dev') and recreate it. This forces BTP to clear all metadata associated with that space.

BTP Support/Feedback: If even space deletion fails, you can use the "Give Feedback" button in the BTP Cockpit. Trial support is limited, but the engineering team often runs scripts to clear these stuck GUIDs.

Quick Tip: Next time, always delete the Service Bindings and HDI instances before deleting the main SAP HANA Cloud instance to avoid this sync issue.

Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Proxy error GENERAL_ERROR Error duing proxy processin...
entry.content:
Dear @Janarthanan,

Recommended checks for the eDocument Cockpit error:

1. The error pattern is documented by SAP in Note 3695814 and the same symptom includes No users assigned for recipient (/EDUBL ALL). In Public Cloud recipient-to-user assignment is handled in the Assign Recipients to Users app / Message Monitoring setup

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/576fa8d8c74443b18c622068b6f55fa4.html

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003695814

2. If this is a Belgium e-invoicing scenario (based on screen yes), verify the country-specific /EDUBL value mappings in the Configure Value Mapping app, including TAX_EXEMPT_CODE, SALES_ORG_PART_FUNC and UNIT_OF_MEASURE_CODE. Missing or inconsistent mappings can cause submission issues

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/fba518519f834ed99011786d831ee504/fe7ced984bb04fa48cda8c043bc023ff.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/fba518519f834ed99011786d831ee504/cb775298bb694555a6e5f37df59710b3.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/fba518519f834ed99011786d831ee504/0885bf37614c47a494dbf81c0098473e.html

3. Also verify the DRC Cloud communication setup, including SSL client certificate service bindings as this is a relevant troubleshooting area for eDocument submission issues

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/creating-service-bindings-using-ssl-client-certificate?version=Cloud

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Composable Storefront – ASM - data isolation true - B2B site
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working with SAP Commerce Cloud + Composable Storefront (Spartacus) and trying to enable ASM (Assisted Service Mode).

I’m facing two issues:

  • Red banner: Cannot find user with propertyValue 'current'
  • 401 on /assistedservicewebservices/customers/search
  • Green banner: You have successfully signed out. (even if I just logged in)

I’d like to understand what I’m doing wrong.

Configuration

OAuth client

I created the ASM client with this Impex:

INSERT_UPDATE OAuthClientDetails; clientId[unique=true] ; public ; authorities ; scope ; authorizedGrantTypes ; registeredRedirectUri ; loginPageUri ; accessTokenValiditySeconds[default=99999999999]
; asm_client ; true ; ROLE_CLIENT ; basic ; authorization_code,refresh_token ; https://localhost:4000,https://localhost:4000/cy,https://localhost:4000/{mysite} ;

Employee (ASM agent)

$defaultPassword=1234
INSERT_UPDATE Employee; name ; groups(uid)[mode = append] ; password[default = $defaultPassword] ; uid[unique = true]
; Sales employee ; salesemployeegroup,orgemployeegroup,asagentsalesmanagergroup,customermanagergroup,employeegroup ; ; salesemployee@nakano.com

I can log into Backoffice successfully with this user.

ASM login flow

I go to: https://localhost:4000/{mySite}/en/RON/?asm=true

I log in as the ASM agent.

After login:

I see the red banner: Cannot find user with propertyValue 'current'

I also see the green banner: You have successfully signed out.

However:

  • I can see the ASM UI
  • I can enter a customer to impersonate

 

But when I try to search customers, this call: https://localhost:9002/assistedservicewebservices/customers/search?baseSite=... returns: 401 Unauthorized

 

What am I missing to make ASM fully functional in Composable Storefront?

Is there:

  • Any additional configuration required at Composable Storefront level?
  • Any specific OAuth client configuration required for ASM (e.g. different authorities, roles, scopes)?

  • Any required OCC or security configuration that must be enabled for /assistedservicewebservices/** when using Composable?
  • Any clarification on what is required to make the full ASM flow work correctly would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

Stefania

entry.title: Parallel Class Assignment on Functional Location
entry.content:
1. Issue / Problem Statement

It has been observed that the technical object classification values currently do not meet the expected data quality standards in terms of

  • Completeness
  • Consistency
  • Accuracy
  • Uniqueness

Due to the large volume of legacy data and inconsistencies across multiple sources, the business has not been able to complete the required data cleansing activities within the planned timeline during the S4 HANA transformation.

At the same time, the project team does not want to lose the existing legacy classification data, as it may still provide valuable reference information for historical context, engineering analysis, or future asset validation.

Therefore, there is a need for a mechanism to retain the legacy data without impacting the operational master data quality of the new S4 system.

2. Proposed Solution

Parallel Class Assignment on Functional Location in SAP

To address the above challenge, the proposed solution is to introduce a parallel classification structure on Functional Locations (FLOC) in SAP.

This approach will allow the following:

  • Migration of existing legacy Technical Object Classification values into a separate reference class
  • Preservation of historical data without interfering with the new standardized classification model
  • Future reference and traceability for engineering and maintenance teams

Key Concept

Two classification layers will exist for the same Functional Location:

Classification Type

Purpose

Standard SAP FLOC Class

Clean, validated classification used for operational processes

Bio+ Reference Class (Parallel Class)

Legacy Bio+ data stored for historical reference

This ensures:

  • Clean operational data
  • No loss of legacy information
  • Reduced dependency on immediate data cleansing

3. Benefits of the Approach

✔ Preserves legacy technical data
✔ Enables progressive data cleansing over time
✔ Maintains clean operational classification for SAP processes
✔ Supports future engineering analysis and traceability
✔ Avoids project delays caused by data quality remediation

4. Implementation Considerations

  • Create a separate class type for reference data (if required)
  • Define parallel class assignment to functional location.
  • Migrate Bio+ TOC values into reference characteristics
  • Restrict usage to display/reference only
  • Ensure governance rules to prevent operational usage

5. Project Impact

Area

Impact

Go-Live

Not go-live critical

Scope

Implemented via the scope management process

Business Impact

Minimal operational impact

Data Strategy

Enables controlled transition to clean master data

Functional Location with Standard Class “003” assignment:

Functional Location with “Z03” Class assignment:

SAP Configuration of “Z03” Class for Functional Location:

SAP Configuration of “Z03” Class for Functional Location:

“Z03” Class Organizational Areas Assignment:

Class Type "Z03" Class A11 Organizational Areas G, R assigned:

In Characteristic A0957 Class type “Z03” assignment in Restrictions:

Key Takeaways

In many asset-intensive transformations, legacy technical data rarely meets the required data quality standards at the time of system migration. Rather than losing valuable historical information, a parallel class assignment on functional location (FLOC) in SAP offers a pragmatic solution. This approach enables organizations to preserve legacy technical object classification data as reference while maintaining clean, standardized master data for operational processes. It balances data governance with business practicality, allowing progressive data cleansing without delaying project timelines. A simple yet powerful strategy to protect legacy knowledge while building a future-ready asset data foundation in SAP EAM.

#SAP #SAPAssetManagement #EAM #MasterData #DataGovernance #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: Re: How to Add "Document Header Text" in Free Selection o...
entry.content:
In F110, the Free Selection functionality in the payment program is designed based on specific logical databases. Consequently, selection is only possible for fields from the BKPF (Header) or BSEG (Item) tables that also exist within the BSIK (Vendor Open Items) or BSID (Customer Open Items) tables.

As the BKTXT (Document Header Text) field is not contained within these index tables (BSIK/BSID), it is currently not possible to perform a selection based on this field.

Furthermore, please note that incorporating this field into the standard selection logic is not intended by SAP. Adding non-indexed header fields like BKTXT to the payment selection process would result in a significant negative impact on system performance during the payment run, and unfortunately there is no standard solution for this requirement.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Embedding Attachments in Belgium Peppol eInvoices
entry.content:
Introduction

Belgium's B2B eInvoicing mandate requires invoices to be transmitted via the Peppol network in UBL 2.1 format. Unlike some electronic document formats that support external document references, Peppol transmits the invoice as a single UBL XML file. This means there is no separate transmission channel for supporting documents — any attachment that needs to accompany the invoice must be embedded directly into the XML.

This blog post describes how to use the BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR to read attachments from the GOS Attachment List of a billing document and embed them into the UBL XML, so they are transmitted together with the Peppol invoice or credit note.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the on-premise implementation using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ and are not covered here.

How Attachments Are Embedded in UBL XML / BIS Billing 3.0

In UBL 2.1, supporting documents are referenced using the AdditionalDocumentReference element. For Peppol, the attachment content is Base64-encoded and placed directly inside the EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element. The following attributes are required:

  • mimeCode — the MIME type of the attached file (e.g., application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
  • filename — the name of the attached file

The DocumentTypeCode element with value 130 is used to indicate that the document is a supporting reference and no attachment, in line with EN16931 / BG-24.

A typical XML structure looks as follows:

<cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>
  <cbc:ID>42010AEF83EE1FE187BE825366FAF87F</cbc:ID>
  <cac:Attachment>
    <cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject
      mimeCode="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"
      filename="Sample Timesheet.xlsx">
      [Base64-encoded content]
    </cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject>
  </cac:Attachment>
</cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>

Since Peppol does not support external URI references as a transmission mechanism, embedding is the only viable approach to include attachments in a Peppol invoice.

Prerequisites

Functional / Configuration Side

  • The Belgium EU eInvoice scenario is configured and active. You can verify this in the Manage Electronic Documents app, where the billing document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process.
  • The attachment has been uploaded to the GOS Attachment List of the relevant billing document (via Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment).
  • Keep individual attachments below 3 MB. While Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 does not impose a strict size limit, large embedded files can cause transmission issues. As a general guideline, keep the total embedded attachment size well below 3 MB.

ABAP / Developer Side

  • BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR is available in the system.
  • A custom BAdI implementation will be created as described in the next section.
  • The relevant filter values are:
    • COUNTRY = BE
    • GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)

Implementation: BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR

Create the BAdI Implementation

  1. Go to transaction SE18 and search for BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR.
  2. Create a new implementation , e.g. ZEI_EDOC_ADAPTOR_BE.
  3. Set the filter values as follows:

Filter Value

COUNTRYBE
GENERIC_FILTER(leave empty)
  1. Navigate to method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA and add the implementation described in the following sections.
  2. Activate the BAdI implementation.

Check Source Type and Retrieve the Billing Document

The first step inside the method is to verify that the source document is an SD billing document and to retrieve the billing document number. If the source type is not SD_INVOICE or the billing document number is initial, the method exits without further processing.

DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

" Only process SD Invoice source documents
IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

  " Retrieve SD source data including the billing document header
  io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

  " Only proceed if a valid billing document number is available
  IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

    " ... (attachment retrieval and embedding — see below)

  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

Retrieve Attachments via cl_odata_cv_attachment_api

Attachments are retrieved using the cl_odata_cv_attachment_api class. This API reads all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List for a given business object. In this case, the object type is VBRK (billing document header) and the object key is the billing document number.

DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
      lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
      ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

" Get singleton instance of the attachment API
lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

" Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
TRY.
    lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
      EXPORTING
        iv_objecttype          = 'VBRK'
        iv_objectkey           = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
      IMPORTING
        et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
    ).
  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
    MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
ENDTRY.

The result is an internal table lt_attachments containing one entry per attachment, including the file content, filename, and MIME type.

Filter Attachments (Optional)

By default, all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List are retrieved and will be embedded into the XML. If you need to restrict which attachments are included — for example, to include only PDF files — you can filter the internal table before processing:

" Example: only include PDF attachments
DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mimetype NE 'application/pdf'.

Size Reminder: Before embedding, consider the total size of all attachments. As noted in the prerequisites, keep the combined embedded content below 3 MB to avoid potential transmission issues.

Embed Attachments into the UBL XML

The method handles two document types: invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM). The correct path is determined by checking iv_interface_id. For each attachment, the following fields are mapped into the AdditionalDocumentReference structure of the UBL XML:

Attachment API Field UBL XML Element

file_idID
filenameDocumentDescription + filename attribute
mimetypemimeCode attribute
attachment_contentEmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject content

The DocumentTypeCode is set to 130 to indicate a document reference per EN16931 / BG-24.

Invoice path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code (e.g., '130' for attachment)
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

Credit Note path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.
          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

Complete Source Code

METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
      " ========================================================================
      " Retrieve and embed attachments for SD Invoice / Credit Note
      " Only executed when processing an SD billing document
      " ========================================================================

      DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

      " Check if this is an SD Invoice source
      IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

        " Get the SD source data (billing document header and items)
        io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

        " Only proceed if we have a valid billing document number
        IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 1: Retrieve attachment IDs and content from GOS
          " ====================================================================
          DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
                lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
                ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

          " Get singleton instance of the attachment API
          lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

          " Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
          " Object type = 'VBRK', Object key = billing document number (10 chars)
          TRY.
              lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
                EXPORTING
                  iv_objecttype  = 'VBRK'
                  iv_objectkey   = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
                IMPORTING
                  et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
              ).
            CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
              " Log or handle error if attachment retrieval fails
              MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
              RETURN.
          ENDTRY.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 2: Filter attachments (optional)
          " ====================================================================
          " TODO: Add your attachment filter logic here if needed.
          " For example, you may want to exclude certain file types or only
          " include attachments with a specific document type code.
          " Example:
          "   DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mime_type NE 'application/pdf'.
          " ====================================================================

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 3: Embed attachments into the UBL XML structure
          " ====================================================================
          FIELD-SYMBOLS:
            <ls_out_struct_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
            <ls_out_struct_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
            <fs_attachment>         TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          " Determine if this is an Invoice or Credit Note
          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF. " vbeln is not initial

      ENDIF. " source_type = SD_INVOICE

      " ========================================================================
      " Continue with any other custom logic below...
      " ======================================================================== 
 ENDMETHOD.

Result and Verification

Once the BAdI implementation is active, you can verify the end-to-end flow using the following steps.

Step 1: Add an Attachment to the Billing Document

Open the billing document in change mode (e.g., via VF02 or the Billing Documents app). Navigate to Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment and upload your file. The attachment will appear in the GOS Attachment List.

Step 2: Verify the Document in Manage Electronic Documents

Open the Manage Electronic Documents app and locate the billing document. The document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process with status Created (In Process).

Step 3: Preview the XML

Select the document and choose Display/Preview XML. In the XML output, locate the AdditionalDocumentReference block. You should see:

  • The ID populated with the GOS file ID
  • The DocumentDescription containing the filename
  • The EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element with the correct mimeCode, filename, and Base64-encoded file content

 

Summary

In this blog post, we covered how to embed GOS attachments into a Belgium Peppol eInvoice XML using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR on SAP S/4HANA on-premise.

Key takeaways:

  • Peppol transmits invoices as a single UBL XML file — embedding is the only option for including attachments
  • Attachments can be retrieved from the GOS Attachment List using cl_odata_cv_attachment_api
  • The sample implementation handles both invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM) via the iv_interface_id parameter
  • BAdI filter: COUNTRY = BE, GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)
  • Keep total embedded attachment size below 3 MB to avoid transmission issues
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP Environment, use EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD — backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content:
The log is not sufficient, it only shows the error message. (And the fact that "Default trace level = none".) For this problem, an RFC trace is required. (Which dumps the raw data received over the network from the SAP system.)

In the network dump, we can then see whether SAP indeed sends broken data (the error message looks like it) and then let the tRFC experts on SAP side investigate further.

But before investing any more time into this, the first step should be to update NCo to 3.1.6. It doesn't make sense to investigate such a complex low level problem, when one of the central components is completely out-of-date....
For example, one other idea that comes to mind: the data corruption could also be caused by a byte-order problem: Windows has little-endian byte order, and if the SAP system is on Unix, it would have big-endian byte order. And I think between NCo 3.0.22 and 3.1.6 (which is a total of 11 patch levels, 3.0.23 - 3.0.26 and 3.1.0 - 3.1.6) there have been bug fixes in the area of incorrect byte order handling, and also with regards to other possible data corruptions. I quickly checked the SAP notes for these NCo patch levels and found at least 3 critical bug fixes with regards to incorrect table data, tRFC handling and data corruption:

  • 3.1.4, note 3410323, point 3: "In an NCo server application, table parameter data contains "garbage" characters in some fields."
  • 3.1.2, note 3293530, point 2: "Function module data sent with tRFC, is not available in the backend"
  • 3.0.24, note 2956253, point 4: "An exception occurs that is similar to: SAP.Middleware.Connector.RfcInvalidStateException: index 30 out of range"
    Note that we are getting a very similar error here: "line 0 out of range"!

So I think the chances are quite high that you are running into one of these problems that have already been fixed long time ago. (After all, NCo 3.0.22 is already 7 years old now...)

entry.title: Re: SAP Field Logistics: Next Generation Kitting & Staging for MRO Industries
entry.content:
Hello @MRO-Tim-McLain, when we do FL kitting at remote or in the single plant for local held stock scenarios like SP11, SP13, SP14;

  1. Once the physical picking is completed and the stage button is selected -> the inventory is 311 transfered from the Main Store SLOC Bin to a virtual Staging SLOC.  At this point, per material a location ID can be assigned to track where the item is being held.  Items can have different Location ID's.  
  2. If Dropped is being used, the inventory is then also 311 transfered from Staged to Droped is also virtual SLOC.  261 GI is completed from the Staging or Dropped SLOC depending on the final process. At this point, per material a location ID can be assigned to track where the item is being held.  Items can have different Location ID's

When the kitting is being augemented with base kitting and depending on the FL Supply Process version being used, generally speaking for SP12 and SP15;

  1. After GR at remote as there is no stock and the "auto staging" is active, the items will show in the Staged view with the appropiate status.  At this point, per material a location ID can be assigned to track where the item is being held.  Items can have different Location ID's.
  2. If Dropped is being used, then the transfer from Staged to Droped is also virtual status update.  The Field Logstics "Issue to Order" is completed from the Staging or Dropped SLOC depending on the final process. At this point, per material a location ID can be assigned to track where the item is being held.  Items can have different Location ID'

The screen shots for this are the same as above.  As you can see when it comes to kitting in FL we dont treat the kit as a "something".  A kit for us group of what is currently needed for the job - we can then remove this from inventory and track the location as a group or individually. We also allow for the issue at anytime and also material by material.  When kitting with full inventory management ie SP11, SP13, SP14, we do reccommend you set up your MRP area accordingly, the Stagged and Dropped SLOC should be excluded from MRP.  Also to note - EWM is supported in the above scenaro for the main store only but the Stage and Drop is always IM.

I hope this diagram best describes the movements

When it comes to seeing this - the Kitting App has kit status on number of item to staged, dropped and issued. 

This information is also visable here in the logistics tracking.  The first 3 items match the above example with the items not yet staged and dropped not showing green:
